Scenario.title: SolarWinds-Inspired Supply Chain Attack on Healthcare Infrastructure
Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Complex A sophisticated supply chain attack leveraging a compromised medical software vendor to infiltrate healthcare institutions, ultimately leading to data manipulation and operational disruption through a multi-stage infiltration campaign.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Intellectual property theft and operational disruption

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: ["Internet-facing assets", "Shared DB or directory", "Vulnerability", "Custom APIs", "Web server services such as emails and databases"]
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: ["Compromised and weak credentials", "Vulnerability exploit", "Web", "DNS Tunneling", "RDP"]

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: Adversaries collected public data on healthcare providers and medical device vendors to identify supply chain dependencies, focusing on third-party software suppliers with internet-exposed update servers.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: A counterfeit organization was created to mimic a legitimate medical software vendor, allowing adversaries to inject malicious code into the update server used by healthcare clients.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2025-0111 [CWE-287]: Authentication bypass via stolen credentials in software update API
stage.description: Stolen vendor credentials were used to authenticate to healthcare targets' update channels, granting access to deploy malicious payloads through legitimate software distribution mechanisms.

stage.name: Execution
stage.surface: Web server services such as emails and databases
stage.kev: CVE-2023-36584 [CWE-295]: Improper validation of certificate chains in update verification process
stage.description: A spoofed software update containing the MEDSTAR backdoor was deployed via the compromised vendor's update server, exploiting weak certificate validation to bypass integrity checks.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: A hidden Windows service was created to maintain persistent access, configured to execute periodically and evade detection by mimicking legitimate system processes.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Weakly protected administrative interfaces in medical devices were exploited to escalate privileges, granting access to sensitive patient data and control systems.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: Attackers used TLS-encrypted C2 communications disguised as HTTPS traffic to avoid detection, mimicking legitimate telehealth platform traffic patterns.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: Domain controller credentials were extracted using NTDS.dit dumping techniques, enabling lateral movement across the healthcare network.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: Internal network scanning identified critical assets including PACS systems and unpatched IoT medical devices, mapping out high-value targets for exploitation.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: Compromised workstations were used as pivots to access isolated segments containing protected health information (PHI) via RDP tunnels.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: Patient records and clinical trial data were compressed and aggregated for efficient exfiltration, reducing detection risk during transmission.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: Stolen data was stealthily transferred via encoded DNS queries to external command-and-control servers, evading traditional network monitoring tools.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Critical medical records were altered, life-support device configurations were disrupted, and PHI was leaked to cause maximum operational chaos and reputational harm.

Scenario.summary: This complex supply chain attack began with reconnaissance targeting healthcare providers, followed by compromising a third-party vendor's update infrastructure. Using stolen credentials, adversaries executed malicious updates that established persistence and escalated privileges to manipulate critical systems, ultimately causing widespread disruption through data tampering and exfiltration.
